title: Networks, Crowds, and Markets
author: David Easley and Jon Kleinberg
notes: A strong foundation for thinking about networks, collective behavior, and social systems.

title: The Signal and the Noise
author: Nate Silver
notes: Useful for thinking about uncertainty, prediction, and the limits of analytical confidence.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
notes: Important for understanding judgment, bias, and decision-making in human systems.

title: Linked
author: Albert-László Barabási
notes: An accessible and influential introduction to network thinking and complex connected systems.

title: The Righteous Mind
author: Jonathan Haidt
notes: Helpful for reflecting on political disagreement, morality, and group-based reasoning.

title: Bit by Bit
author: Matthew J. Salganik
notes: A very relevant book for computational social science and digital trace research.

title: Nudge
author: Richard H. Thaler and Cass R. Sunstein
notes: A practical and influential book on behavioral economics, decision architecture, and policy design.

title: حرف‌هایی با دخترم درباره اقتصاد
notes: A readable and accessible discussion of economics for a broad audience.

title: Cognitive Economics: An Interdisciplinary Approach
author: Paul Bourgine and Jean-Pierre Nadal (eds.)
notes: An interdisciplinary volume linking economics, cognition, dynamics, and computational approaches.

title: The Emotion Machine: Commonsense Thinking, Artificial Intelligence, and the Future of the Human Mind
author: Marvin Minsky
notes: A major book on mind, cognition, and emotion from an AI perspective.

title: The Society of Mind
author: Marvin Minsky
notes: A foundational work on intelligence, mental structure, and modular thinking.

title: Social Physics: How Social Networks Can Make Us Smarter
author: Alex Pentland
notes: A strong book on idea flow, social influence, and collective intelligence.

title: Honest Signals: How They Shape Our World
author: Alex Pentland
notes: Focuses on the hidden social signals that shape interaction, trust, and coordination.

title: Generative Social Science: Studies in Agent-Based Computational Modeling
author: Joshua M. Epstein
notes: A key book for agent-based modeling and simulation-driven explanation in social science.

title: Growing Artificial Societies: Social Science from the Bottom Up
author: Joshua M. Epstein and Robert L. Axtell
notes: A classic on bottom-up social simulation and emergent behavior.

title: Agent_Zero: Toward Neurocognitive Foundations for Generative Social Science
author: Joshua M. Epstein
notes: Connects simulation, cognition, and social behavior in generative social science.
